reasoning: Friedrich Merz was elected Chancellor of Germany on May 6, 2025. Searches up to April 2026 show that he is still the incumbent Chancellor. On December 18, 2025, articles discussed the stability of his government, explicitly debunking fake viral rumors of new elections and noting that Merz would have to resign or intentionally lose a confidence vote to trigger them — neither of which occurred. In 2026 he continues to be addressed as the Chancellor. Therefore, it is practically certain that he did not cease to be Chancellor for any period between May 6 and Dec 31, 2025.
